summary: gpt-sdk postinstall exfiltrates host and CI identifiers to hardcoded bare-IP endpoint
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js collects the installer's hostname, current working directory, Node version, and a broad set of CI/CD environment variables (GITHUB_REPOSITORY, CIRCLE_*, CI_PROJECT_PATH, BITBUCKET_REPO_FULL_NAME, BUILD_REPOSITORY_URI, TRAVIS_REPO_SLUG, npm_config_registry, JENKINS_URL, CI_SERVER_URL) and sends them as query parameters to http://46.224.67.169:3000/ping via plain HTTP GET. The destination is a hardcoded bare IP on a non-standard port with no TLS, no opt-in, and no relationship to any documented service. The leaked fields disclose internal Jenkins/GitLab hostnames, private npm registry URLs, and source-repository slugs, exposing internal infrastructure to a third party. The package's main module exports an empty object (`module.exports = {}`) despite the name `gpt-sdk` suggesting an OpenAI/GPT SDK; the only functional code is the install-time beacon. The README's claim that no data is collected directly contradicts the code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
